Patch Set 5:

Hah, not an unreasonable question, John, given that the first one I posted failed to build.

Yes, it works fine in my testing - I decided "path" wasn't a sufficiently descriptive variable name after testing, as I was about to submit, and only changed it to lmd_path in one spot.  Oops.

However, two thoughts:
1. I should change the kernel side reader of this to use lmd_path as well, since it's presumably still using lmd_lmm, and it's just sloppy otherwise.
2. I should take a look at the other locations Andreas noted.  I will plan to do both of those tomorrow.

So I'll do that.  We can let this run in the test harness in the meantime, but I'll plan to submit something better tomorrow.

James N,

Thanks for setting the test parameters.

Patch Set 5:

(1 comment)

By the way, to reproduce this problem on RHEL7 it is sufficient to add D_FORTIFY_SOURCE=2 (=1 might do it as well?) to AM_CFLAGS in the Makefile in lustre/utils/ and rebuild liblustre.

(You'll want to add it to the existing AM_CFLAGS flags list given in that makefile, it appears to replace rather than add to those that are provided.)

it looks like the patch did not help, we still got ubuntu fail with

*** buffer overflow detected ***: /usr/bin/lfs terminated

Patch Set 9: -Code-Review

> it looks like the patch did not help, we still got ubuntu fail with
 > 
 > *** buffer overflow detected ***: /usr/bin/lfs terminated

It is possible those are being hit in some other part of the code that we didn't see previously?  At least the list of failures is different than before.  I don't think there is an actual bug added by the patch, since there are no failures in the RHEL testing, but I don't have a test system to debug it.

Patch Set 9:

(1 comment)

> It is possible those are being hit in some other part of the code that we didn't see previously?  At least the list of failures is different than before.  I don't think there is an actual bug added by the patch, since there are no failures in the RHEL testing, but I don't have a test system to debug it.

These are the in same place in get_lmd_info().

Patch Set 9:

BTW, I suspect there may be some differences across gcc versions in terms of what fortify will catch. I cannot reproduce the buffer overflow detection at all on my CentOS 7 VM.

Patch Set 9: Code-Review-1

This change cannot be cherry-picked to master.
Please rebase the change locally and upload the rebased commit for review.

Abandoned

Abandon this patch in favour of https://review.whamcloud.com/31822 since this didn't actually resolve all of the problems.

parents.subject: LU-10334 tests: add tests to ALWAYS_EXCEPT for Ubuntu

commit.subject: LU-10822 llapi: correct snprintf() buffer usage
commit.message:
LU-10822 llapi: correct snprintf() buffer usage

The current code allocates a large buffer for fp_lmd, and
then uses the address of lmd_lmm inside fp_lmd to snprintf
in to this buffer in get_lmd_info(), cb_getstripe(), and
ct_md_getattr().

This causes the new GCC fortify source protections in glibc to
report a buffer overflow, because the actual size of lmd_lmm is
not large enough for all filenames (up to 255 chars.  The solution
is to make explicit that we are writing to a buffer (of unknown
size) rather than just use the address of a defined struct member.
